description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer featuring a robust, rectangular black metal head with slightly beveled edges and a flat striking surface on both ends. The head appears solid and dense, likely made of forged steel, and shows a subtle marbled texture. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and constructed from light-colored wood, displaying a natural grain and some visible wear, suggesting frequent use. The junction between the head and handle is tight, with the handle inserted deeply into the head for stability. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing durability and effective force transfer for tasks such as breaking concrete or driving stakes. No visible branding, logos, or additional attachments are present on the hammer.
description: A sturdy sledgehammer with a black metal rectangular head and a straight, light wooden handle, showing signs of use and wear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the rectangular black metal head.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the narrow face of the hammer head and the circular end of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full handle and the other side of the rectangular head.
description_view_3: View from the handle end, focusing on the handle's circular cross-section and the width of the hammer head.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle, cylindrical, with visible grain and some wear; no ergonomic shaping or grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledgehammer head, double-faced, made of black metal with flat striking surfaces and slightly beveled edges.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible wear on the wooden handle, including discoloration and minor surface marks; no major damage or rust on the head.
